title: viola howellii
intro: Viola howellii, also known as Howell's violet, is a rare species of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae). It is native to the western United States and is recognized by its small, delicate purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Viola howellii, such as teas and tinctures, have been used to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from symptoms associated with colds and fever.
